chapter1.article_135bis_understood — NOT YET DECLARED. Referenced in story.txt §Beat 12 as gating Cula's strongest court arguments. Must be added to state.gd (bool, default false) and chapter1.json new_state_flags.

murrow_archive_narration: 1 (Laugh): 'He served old number seven. He knew the address was twelve. Service is invalid.' — flat delivery of a conclusion that demolishes the landlord's case. 'Nine days remaining.' said like a stock inventory.

2 (Clever): Three clauses apply real fabricated-KPC doctrine (Article 135-bis § 2) to specific facts in evidence. The landlord's anticipated defense (third-party acceptance cures service) is preempted before it is filed.
3 (Alive): Every line in Murrow's terse-instructional register. Three-word declaratives. No adjectives. The rhythm is an audit, not a lecture.
4 (Clear): After this state the player knows: (a) the legal hook, (b) the deadline, (c) why the likely counter-argument fails.
5 (Future-proof): No content that would break on Ch2–5 reread. 'Nine days remaining' is a fixed historical fact in Ch1.
murrow_archive_dwell_archival: 1 (Laugh): Murrow gives the shelf coordinates for a statute. The joke is the completeness and the year.
2 (Clever): Calls back the 'second shelf, blue spine' from murrow_first_meeting — the binder Murrow named there is the same binder.
3 (Alive): Pure Murrow register — spatial, archival, unfazed.
4 (Clear): Satisfies Cula's curiosity (and the player's) about why Murrow knows this article.
murrow_archive_dwell_prior: 1 (Laugh): Deadpan progression — 'once / the motion failed / the case settled after' — the dry humor is in the sequence and the gap between each answer.
2 (Clever): Timeliness is real KPC doctrine. The prior use failing on the same clause Cula is now inside reinforces that 'nine days remaining' is not just procedural flavor.
3 (Alive): Murrow's two-word answers are the character.
4 (Clear): The implication — being inside the window matters; Murrow has seen it not matter — lands without explanation.
5 (Future-proof): No client named; no detail that would bind Ch5.
murrow_archive_dwell_next: 1 (Laugh): The checklist ends with a printer joke. Mrożek-surface register. The printer's feelings are a running gag that never gets old in this office.
2 (Clever): The checklist items (Crab service / Whimsy fair-hearing / filing window) are the actual Beat 10 readiness check items, so this line is simultaneously funny and accurate.
3 (Alive): The printer flicker breaks the otherwise purely mechanical tone in a way that feels like Murrow relaxing for one sentence.
4 (Clear): The player knows exactly what the next three tasks are.
5 (Future-proof): Printer running joke is a permanent fixture per AGENTS.md §Humor rules.

_scope: Beat 9 Archive Room narration (new states). To promote: append these four states to murrow.json's states array before the idle_flavor key. Do not modify existing states.

states._comment: Beat 9. Fires when Murrow is in the Archive Room with Cula (Halina has left; met_murrow and halina_met are both true; archive research is not yet done). Murrow narrates Article 135-bis § 2 KPC clause by clause, applying each clause to Halina's facts now in evidence. Ends with options block (chapter1.murrow_beat9_dwell, chain:true) for Cula's three dwell choices. Murrow uses bare 'Cula' throughout (private context, post-befriending).
lines: The binder. Article 135-bis, section two of the Code of Civil Procedure.
Service is invalid when the address used has been renumbered to the knowledge of the serving party.
The building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. The landlord authorized the renumbering — his countersignature is on the two thousand and nineteen renewal.
He served old number seven. He knew the address was twelve. Service is invalid.
A hearing held on invalid service shall, on motion of the party not properly served, be set aside, provided the motion is filed within fourteen days of actual notice.
She received the notice on the twenty-eighth of April. Today is the fifth of May.
Yes. Third clause. Appearance of a third party at the served address does not cure service unless that party held the express authority of the served party.
The current occupant of number seven is not her authorized agent. The landlord's objection fails before he files it.

choices.text: How did you find this article?
Have you used this clause before?

states._comment: Beat 9 dwell — archival-aside register. Murrow gives shelf coordinates for the statute. Calls back the 'second shelf, blue spine' from murrow_first_meeting. on_dismiss sets both Beat 9 completion flags.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: How did you find this article?

lines: Second shelf from the left. Blue spine. It has been there since two thousand and eight.

states._comment: Beat 9 dwell — terse-instructional register. Murrow confirms one prior use; the motion failed on timeliness. The dry progression (once / failed / settled after) is the joke. Sets both Beat 9 completion flags.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Have you used this clause before?

lines: The motion failed on timeliness. The client received actual notice too late.
The case settled. After the motion failed.

states._comment: Beat 9 dwell — practical bridge to Beat 10. Murrow gives the readiness checklist (Crab, Whimsy, filing window). Printer gag ends the scene in Mrożek-surface register. Sets both Beat 9 completion flags. After this state fires, the existing murrow.json court_readiness_check state is the next meaningful Murrow interaction.
lines: Crab confirms service. Whimsy confirms the fair-hearing argument. You confirm the filing window with the clerk. Then we establish whether the printer will let us leave.
